titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Arabic Film Poster Collection
titlestmt.author: compiled by Zeina Alhendi Alattar

controlnote.p: This encoded finding aid is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday September 2, 2020 at 09:55

descriptivenote.p: The collection is mostly in Arabic, with some French.

did.abstract: The collection is comprised of 1284 film posters, 156 film lobby cards, and 96 film press books from Arab countries. The majority of the items are from Egypt, but include pieces from Algeria, Iraq, Lebanon, Syria and other countries.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from al-Furat Publishing and Distribution Company, Beirut, Lebanon, and transferred to Manuscripts and Archives by the curator of the Near Eastern Collection, 2006.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: While this collection as a whole is available for research, parts of it may be restricted due to law, university policy or fragility. Any restricted material will be noted as such.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Copyright status for collection materials is unknown. Transmission or reproduction of materials protected by U.S. Copyright Law (Title 17, U.S.C.) beyond that allowed by fair use requires the written permission of the copyright owners. Works not in the public domain cannot be commercially exploited without permission of the copyright owners. Responsibility for any use rests exclusively with the user.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Arabic Film Poster Collection. Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged in five series: I. Posters, 1935-2005. II. Lobby Cards, 1948-1993. III. Press Books, 1954-1989. IV. Half Sheets, 1949-1974. V. Fragile Posters - Egypt, 1956-1961.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection is comprised of 1284 film posters, 156 film lobby cards, and 96 film press books from Arab countries. The majority of the items are from Egypt, but include pieces from Algeria, Iraq, Lebanon, Syria and other countries.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The series is arranged alphabetically by country. When viewing, researchers should maintain the established order of the posters within each box.

altformavail.head: Existence and Location of Copies
altformavail.p: A copy of this material is available in digital form from Manuscripts and Archives. Contact Manuscripts and Archives at mssa.assist@yale.edu to request access to the digital copy.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The series is arranged aphabetically by country.

scopecontent.p: The series is arranged alphabetically by country.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The series is arranged alphabetically by country.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Materials in boxes 95-100 are closed due to their physical condition. Researchers must obtain permission in writing from the Near Eastern Collection Curator to obtain access.
